title: Kinetic Theory of Gases - Chapter Test

questions.questionText: During the melting of a slab of ice at 273 K at atmospheric pressure :
options:
(A) Internal energy of ice-water system remains unchanged.
(B)  Positive work is done by the ice-water system on the atmosphere.
(C)  Positive work is done on the ice-water system by the atmosphere.
(D)  Internal energy of the ice-water system decreases.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A gas is kept in a container having walls which are thermally non-conducting. Initially the gas has a volume of $800 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ and temperature $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The change in temperature when the gas is adiabatically compressed to $200 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ is:

(Take $\gamma=1.5 ; \gamma$ is the ratio of specific heats at constant pressure and at constant volume)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A gun fires a lead bullet of temperature 300 K into a wooden block. The bullet having melting temperature of 600 K penetrates into the block and melts down. If the total heat required for the process is 625 J , then the mass of the bullet is _______ grams. (Latent heat of fusion of lead $=2.5 \times 10^4 \mathrm{JKg}^{-1}$ and specific heat capacity of lead $=125 \mathrm{JKg}^{-1}$ $\left.\mathrm{K}^{-1}\right)$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Match the List - I with List - II

List - I List - II
(A) Pressure varies inversely with volume of an ideal gas. (I) Adiabatic process
(B) Heat absorbed goes partly to increase internal energy and partly to do work. (II) Isochoric process
(C) Heat is neither absorbed nor released by a system. (III) Isothermal process
(D) No work is done on or by a gas. (IV) Isobaric process

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options:
(A)  A-I, B-III, C-II, D-IV
(B) A-I, B-IV, C-II, D-III
(C) A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II
(D) A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An ideal gas initially at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ temperature, is compressed suddenly to one fourth of its volume. If the ratio of specific heat at constant pressure to that at constant volume is $3 / 2$, the change in temperature due to the thermodynamic process is _________ K.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Work done by a Carnot engine operating between temperatures $$127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ and $$27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ is $$2 \mathrm{~kJ}$$. The amount of heat transferred to the engine by the reservoir is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The heat absorbed by a system in going through the given cyclic process is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If the collision frequency of hydrogen molecules in a closed chamber at $$27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ is $$\mathrm{Z}$$, then the collision frequency of the same system at $$127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$ is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The number of air molecules per cm$$^3$$ increased from $$3\times10^{19}$$ to $$12\times10^{19}$$. The ratio of collision frequency of air molecules before and after the increase in number respectively is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A source supplies heat to a system at the rate of $$1000 \mathrm{~W}$$. If the system performs work at a rate of $$200 \mathrm{~W}$$. The rate at which internal energy of the system increases is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A cylinder of fixed capacity of 44.8 litres contains helium gas at standard temperature and pressure. The amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of gas in the cylinder by 20.0$$^\circ$$C will be :

(Given gas constant R = 8.3 JK$$-$$1-mol$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In van der Waal equation $$\left[ {P + {a \over {{V^2}}}} \right]$$ [V $$-$$ b] = RT; P is pressure, V is volume, R is universal gas constant and T is temperature. The ratio of constants $${a \over b}$$ is dimensionally equal to :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two coils require 20 minutes and 60 minutes respectively to produce same amount of heat energy when connected separately to the same source. If they are connected in parallel arrangement to the same source; the time required to produce same amount of heat by the combination of coils, will be ___________ min.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: 300 cal. of heat is given to a heat engine and it rejects 225 cal. If source temperature is 227$$^\circ$$C, then the temperature of sink will be ______________ $$^\circ$$C.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: As per the given figure, two plates A and B of thermal conductivity K and 2 K are joined together to form a compound plate. The thickness of plates are 4.0 cm and 2.5 cm respectively and the area of cross-section is 120 cm2 for each plate. The equivalent thermal conductivity of the compound plate is $$\left( {1 + {5 \over \alpha }} \right)$$ K, then the value of $$\alpha$$ will be ______________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
In the given $P-V$ diagram, a monoatomic gas $\left(\gamma=\frac{5}{3}\right)$ is first compressed adiabatically from state $A$ to state $B$. Then it expands isothermally from state $B$ to state $C$. [Given: $\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{0.6} \simeq 0.5, \ln 2 \simeq 0.7$ ].

Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: (A) The magnitude of the total work done in the process $A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C$ is $144 \mathrm{~kJ}$.
(B) The magnitude of the work done in the process $B \rightarrow C$ is $84 \mathrm{~kJ}$.
(C) The magnitude of the work done in the process $A \rightarrow B$ is $60 \mathrm{~kJ}$.
(D) The magnitude of the work done in the process $C \rightarrow A$ is zero.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The height of victoria falls is 63 m. What is the difference in temperature of water at the top and at the bottom of fall?

[Given 1 cal = 4.2 J and specific heat of water = 1 cal g$$-$$1 $$^\circ$$0C$$-$$1]
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: if the rms speed of oxygen molecules at 0$$^\circ$$C is 160 m/s, find the rms speed of hydrogen molecules at 0$$^\circ$$C.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Match List - I with List - II.

List - I List - II
(A) Isothermal (I) ΔW (work done) = 0
(B) Adiabatic (II) ΔQ (supplied heat) = 0
(C) Isobaric (III) ΔU (change in internal energy) ≠ 0
(D) Isochoric (IV) ΔU = 0

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The helium and argon are put in the flask at the same room temperature (300 K). The ratio of average kinetic energies (per molecule) of helium and argon is:

(Give: Molar mass of helium = 4 g/mol, Molar mass of argon = 40 g/mol)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two cylindrical rods A and B made of different materials, are joined in a straight line. The ratios of lengths, radii and thermal conductivites of these rods are: $\frac{\mathrm{L}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{L}_{\mathrm{B}}}=\frac{1}{2}, \frac{\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{B}}}=2$ and $\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{B}}}=\frac{1}{2}$. The free ends of rods A and B are maintained at 400 K , 200 K , respectively. The temperature of rods interface is ______________ $K$, when equilibrium is established.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
At what temperature a gold ring of diameter 6.230 cm be heated so that it can be fitted on a wooden bangle of diameter 6.241 cm ? Both the diameters have been measured at room temperature (27$$^\circ$$C).

(Given : coefficient of linear thermal expansion of gold $$\alpha$$L = 1.4 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5 K$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A vessel contains 16g of hydrogen and 128g of oxygen at standard temperature and pressure. The volume of the vessel in cm3 is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Starting with the same initial conditions, an ideal gas expands from volume V1 to V2 in three different ways. The work done by the gas is W1 if the process is purely isothermal, W2, if the process is purely adiabatic and W3 if the process is purely isobaric. Then, choose the correct option
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A hole is drilled in a metal sheet. At $$\mathrm{27^\circ C}$$, the diameter of hole is 5 cm. When the sheet is heated to $$\mathrm{177^\circ C}$$, the change in the diameter of hole is $$\mathrm{d\times10^{-3}}$$ cm. The value of d will be __________ if coefficient of linear expansion of the metal is $$1.6\times10^{-5}/^\circ$$C.
questions.subject: Physics
